event: 15-year-old girl
168 cm
57 kg
acute myelogenous leukemia
received standard induction treatment consisting of 2 cycles of chemotherapy according to AML-BFM guidelines
developed pancytopenia
received broad anti-infective treatment for an infection with unknown focus with ciprofloxacin
received broad anti-infective treatment for an infection with unknown focus with linezolid
received broad anti-infective treatment for an infection with unknown focus with meropenem
received broad anti-infective treatment for an infection with unknown focus with tobramycin
received broad anti-infective treatment for an infection with unknown focus with liposomal amphotericin
developed tachycardia (140/min)
developed hypotension (75/41 torr)
developed lactic acidosis
admitted to pediatric intensive care unit
transthoracic echocardiography revealed severely impaired left ventricular ejection fraction (12%) without dilatation of left ventricle (LVIDd 38 mm)
electrocardiogram showed sinus tachycardia with incomplete right bundle branch block
N-terminal prohormone of brain natriuretic peptide (NT-proBNP) was 19,384 pg/ml (normal range 0–153 pg/ml)
high-sensitive troponin T 53.7 pg/ml (normal range 0–14 pg/ml)
started fluid therapy
noradrenaline started
dobutamine started
milrinone started
deep sedation initiated
mechanical ventilation initiated due to respiratory insufficiency
lactic acidosis worsened (16 mmol/l, pH 6.91)
remained hypotensive (50/30 torr)
heart rate 160/min
va-ECMO initiated via cannulation of left femoral artery and vein
9 Fr sheath placed into left superficial femoral artery to ensure distal leg perfusion
va-ECMO applied at blood flow of 3.3 l/min at 3800 rpm
noradrenaline administered (80 µg/min)
vasopressin administered (2 units/h)
levosimendan administered (0.5 mg/h)
hydrocortisone administered (200 mg/d)
residual LVEF remained at about 10–15%
continuous venovenous hemodiafiltration started because of anuric acute kidney injury
anti-infective therapy continued with dose-adjustment of meropenem
anti-infective therapy continued with dose-adjustment of ciprofloxacin
anti-infective therapy continued with dose-adjustment of metronidazole
anti-infective therapy continued with dose-adjustment of cotrimoxazole
anti-infective therapy continued with dose-adjustment of liposomal amphotericin B
anti-infective therapy continued with dose-adjustment of acyclovir
linezolid replaced by vancomycin
procalcitonin was 2.37 ng/ml
C-reactive protein was 2.98 mg/dl
mean arterial pressure declined to 40 torr despite increasing doses of vasopressors
noradrenaline increased to 144 µg/min
vasopressin increased to 4 IE/h
LVEF decreased below 10%
levosimendan infusion stopped after 21 hours without positive effect
acute ischemic injury of non-cannulated leg developed
right femoral artery was dissected in groin
insufficient blood inflow due to low cardiac output causing leg ischemia
lactate levels rose to 25 mmol/l
Dacron conduit sewn on right femoral artery and second arterial cannula introduced through conduit connected to ECMO circuit
impaired lung function
inspiratory oxygen fraction increased up to 0.55 to prevent Harlequin syndrome
procalcitonin levels rose to 42.5 ng/ml
Hickman catheter was explanted as potential septic focus
no causal organism identified
broad-complex tachycardia occurred
treated with esmolol
treated with metoprolol
left ventricular function further decreased
aortic valve ceased to open
second venous cannula placed through right femoral vein into left atrium via percutaneous atrioseptostomy
second venous cannula connected to ECMO circuit by another Y-connector
lactate levels peaked at 29 mmol/l but fell instantly
mean arterial pressure rose from nadir of 30 torr after acidosis leveled out
echocardiography showed left ventricle was unloaded with no residual left ventricular function
therapeutic anticoagulation ensured by unfractionated heparin with target partial thrombin time of 60–80 s
PCT peaked at 80.4 ng/ml
CRP peaked at 7.23 mg/dl
leukocytes remained low at 300/µl
high-sensitive troponin T increased to 343 pg/ml
creatine kinase MB increased to 178 U/l
CK total 577 U/l
all microbial specimens collected during this period remained negative
viral myocarditis was excluded by myocardial biopsy
cardiac systolic function gradually recovered
ECMO cannulas in left femoral vessels were removed
ECMO was completely removed after LVEF recovered to approximately 30%, with mean arterial pressure 60–70 torr
after removal of transseptal cannula, no relevant communication between left and right atrium observed
CVVHDF could be stopped due to normal renal function
leucocyte count recovered to 1000/µl
leucocyte count reached normal values
respirator weaning reached
successful allogenic stem cell transplantation performed
patient discharged to rehabilitation facility, still suffering from severe critical illness polyneuropathy
LVEF recovered to approximately 40% without ventricular dilatation
